guardrails.title: Require Doc Comments
guardrails.body: All classes and functions shall have documentation comments. Use the language's standard format (JSDoc, PHPDoc, docstring, etc.).

guardrails.title: Comment Intent with Structured Prefixes
guardrails.body: MUST: For non-obvious design decisions, workarounds, or security-sensitive handling, you shall leave a single-line comment annotated with one of the structured prefixes so that downstream documentation generation can preserve the intent. Recognized prefixes are exactly `WHY:`, `HACK:`, and `SECURITY:` (uppercase, followed by a colon). MUST: Use `WHY:` for the rationale behind a design decision, `HACK:` for a known temporary workaround or limitation, and `SECURITY:` for security-related judgments. MUST NOT: Do not explain what the code does when the code is self-evident; comment intent (why), not logic (what). MUST: Write the prefixed comment on its own line (optionally indented) using the language's single-line comment syntax (`//` for JS/TS/PHP, `#` for PHP/Python/YAML). End-of-line comments are not preserved even if prefixed. MUST: Lowercase or mixed-case forms (e.g. `why:` or `Why:`) are not recognized and will be stripped. MUST: Write the comment body in the natural language configured as `config.docs.defaultLanguage` so that it matches the generated documentation language.
